rulings.Case Title: KATELYN TAM VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 8, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT MICHAEL BLACK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: LEONINE CAPITAL LLC VS. SAKTI INTERNATIONAL CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Sakti International Corporation'S Motion To Expunge Notice Of Lis Pendens
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 8, 2012, Line 16, Sakti International Corporation'S Motion To Expunge Notice Of Lis Pendens IS Granted. Plaintiff agreed not to file a Lis Pendens. Additionally, Plaintiff has not shown that its claims are probably valid. Plaintiff has not shown that it performed or there are grounds to excuse its performance and that Defendant materially breached the Agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK
